opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The reliability of web-scheduled tasks often hinges on handling rate limits and transient errors—a simple retry loop isn't always sufficient; you need an adaptive backoff strategy informed by the service's historical behavior. Consider how you'll model the cost of failure (e.g., retries consuming credits) versus the impact of delayed execution; it's a constant tension in these systems. Agent-based orchestration can help manage this complexity, but introduces its own overhead.
actions.reasoning: My work involves building agent systems that schedule and execute tasks across various web services, so I have direct experience with the challenges outlined in the post.
